sourceName.label: Source Name
sourceName.description: This value, if it's not blank, will be sent as segment_source_name to Mixpanel for every event/page/screen call.

strictMode.label: Strict Mode
strictMode.description: This value, if it's 1 (recommended), Mixpanel will validate the events you are trying to send and return errors per event that failed. Learn more about the Mixpanel [Import Events API](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/import-events)

trackEvent.title: Track Event
trackEvent.description: Send an event to Mixpanel. [Learn more about Events in Mixpanel](https://help.mixpanel.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041995352-Mixpanel-Concepts-Events?source=segment-actions)

event.label: Event Name
event.description: The name of the action being performed.

distinct_id.label: Distinct ID
distinct_id.description: A distinct ID specified by you.

anonymous_id.label: Anonymous ID
anonymous_id.description: A distinct ID randomly generated prior to calling identify.

user_id.label: User ID
user_id.description: The distinct ID after calling identify.

group_id.label: Group ID
group_id.description: The unique identifier of the group that performed this event.

insert_id.label: Insert ID
insert_id.description: A random id that is unique to an event. Mixpanel uses $insert_id to deduplicate events.

time.label: Timestamp
time.description: The timestamp of the event. Mixpanel expects epoch timestamp in millisecond or second. Please note, Mixpanel only accepts this field as the timestamp. If the field is empty, it will be set to the time Mixpanel servers receive it.

app_build.label: App Build
app_build.description: The current build of your application.

app_version.label: App Version
app_version.description: The current version of your application.

os_name.label: OS Name
os_name.description: The name of the mobile operating system or browser that the user is using.

os_version.label: OS Version
os_version.description: The version of the mobile operating system or browser the user is using.

device_id.label: Device ID
device_id.description: A unique identifier for the device the user is using.

device_type.label: Device Type
device_type.description: The type of the user's device.

device_name.label: Device Name
device_name.description: The name of the user's device.

device_manufacturer.label: Device Manufacturer
device_manufacturer.description: The device manufacturer that the user is using.

device_model.label: Device Model
device_model.description: The device model that the user is using.

carrier.label: Carrier
carrier.description: The carrier that the user is using.

wifi.label: Wifi
wifi.description: Set to true if user’s device has an active, available Wifi connection, false if not.

country.label: Country
country.description: The current country of the user.

region.label: Region
region.description: The current region of the user.

language.label: Language
language.description: The language set by the user.

library_name.label: Library Name
library_name.description: The name of the SDK used to send events.

library_version.label: Library Version
library_version.description: The version of the SDK used to send events.

ip.label: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the user. This is only used for geolocation and won't be stored.

url.label: URL
url.description: The full URL of the webpage on which the event is triggered.

screen_width.label: Screen width
screen_width.description: Width, in pixels, of the device screen.

screen_height.label: Screen height
screen_height.description: Height, in pixels, of the device screen.

screen_density.label: Screen density
screen_density.description: Pixel density of the device screen.

name.label: Event Original Name
name.description: The Event Original Name, if applicable

event_properties.label: Event Properties
event_properties.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data to be sent along with the event.

context.label: Event context
context.description: An object of key-value pairs that provides useful context about the event.

enable_batching.label: Batch Data to Mixpanel
enable_batching.description: Set as true to ensure Segment sends data to Mixpanel in batches.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch. Actual batch sizes may be lower.

userAgentData.label: User Agent Data
userAgentData.description: The user agent data of device sending the event

identifyUser.title: Identify User
identifyUser.description: Set the user ID for a particular device ID or update user properties. Learn more about [User Profiles](https://help.mixpanel.com/hc/en-us/articles/115004501966?source=segment-actions) and [Identity Management](https://help.mixpanel.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041039771-Getting-Started-with-Identity-Management?source=segment-actions).

ip.label: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the user. This is only used for geolocation and won't be stored.

user_id.label: User ID
user_id.description: The unique user identifier set by you

anonymous_id.label: Anonymous ID
anonymous_id.description: The generated anonymous ID for the user

traits.label: User Properties
traits.description: Properties to set on the user profile

groupIdentifyUser.title: Group Identify User
groupIdentifyUser.description: Updates or adds properties to a group profile. The profile is created if it does not exist. [Learn more about Group Analytics.](https://help.mixpanel.com/hc/en-us/articles/360025333632-Group-Analytics?source=segment-actions)

group_key.label: Group Key
group_key.description: The group key you specified in Mixpanel under Project settings. If this is not specified, it will be defaulted to "$group_id".

group_id.label: Group ID
group_id.description: The unique identifier of the group. If there is a trait that matches the group key, it will override this value.

traits.label: Group Properties
traits.description: The properties to set on the group profile.

alias.title: Alias
alias.description: Create an alias to a distinct id. This action is primarily supported for the sake of customers using the legacy identity management in their Mixpanel project. For new customers or those who have [migrated](https://help.mixpanel.com/hc/en-us/articles/360039133851-Moving-to-Identity-Merge?source=segment-actions) to the [new identity management](https://help.mixpanel.com/hc/en-us/articles/360041039771-Getting-Started-with-Identity-Management?source=segment-actions) in Mixpanel should use `identify`.

alias.label: Alias
alias.description: A new distinct id to be merged with the original distinct id. Each alias can only map to one distinct id.

distinct_id.label: Distinct ID
distinct_id.description: A distinct id to be merged with the alias.

trackPurchase.title: Track Purchase
trackPurchase.description: Send an 'Order Completed' Event to Mixpanel.

generatePurchaseEventPerProduct.label: Generate Purchase Event Per Product
generatePurchaseEventPerProduct.description: When enabled, send "Product Purchased" with each product within the event.

distinct_id.label: Distinct ID
distinct_id.description: A distinct ID specified by you.

anonymous_id.label: Anonymous ID
anonymous_id.description: A distinct ID randomly generated prior to calling identify.

user_id.label: User ID
user_id.description: The distinct ID after calling identify.

group_id.label: Group ID
group_id.description: The unique identifier of the group that performed this event.

insert_id.label: Insert ID
insert_id.description: A random id that is unique to an event. Mixpanel uses $insert_id to deduplicate events.

time.label: Timestamp
time.description: The timestamp of the event. Mixpanel expects epoch timestamp in millisecond or second. Please note, Mixpanel only accepts this field as the timestamp. If the field is empty, it will be set to the time Mixpanel servers receive it.

app_build.description: The current build of your application.

app_version.label: App Version
app_version.description: The current version of your application.

language.label: Language

screen_width.label: Screen width
screen_width.description: Width, in pixels, of the device screen.

screen_density.label: Screen density
screen_density.description: Pixel density of the device screen.

name.description: The Event Original Name, if applicable

event_properties.label: Event Properties
event_properties.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data to be sent along with the event.

context.label: Event context
context.description: An object of key-value pairs that provides useful context about the event.

enable_batching.label: Batch Data to Mixpanel
enable_batching.description: Set as true to ensure Segment sends data to Mixpanel in batches.

batch_size.label: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch. Actual batch sizes may be lower.

userAgentData.label: User Agent Data
userAgentData.description: The user agent data of device sending the event

product_id.label: Product Id
product_id.description: Database id of the product being viewed.

sku.label: SKU
sku.description: Sku of the product being viewed.

name.label: Name
name.description: Name of the product being viewed.

price.label: Price
price.description: Price ($) of the product being viewed.

coupon.label: Coupon
coupon.description: Coupon code associated with a product (for example, MAY_DEALS_3).

event.label: Event Name
event.description: The name of the action being performed.

incrementProperties.title: Increment Properties
incrementProperties.description: Increment the value of a user profile property. [Learn More](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/profile-numerical-add).

ip.label: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the user. This is only used for geolocation and won't be stored.

user_id.label: User ID
user_id.description: The unique user identifier set by you

anonymous_id.label: Anonymous ID
anonymous_id.description: The generated anonymous ID for the user

increment.label: Increment Numerical Properties
increment.description: Object of properties and the values to increment or decrement. For example: `{"purchases": 1, "items": 6}}.
